facts.claim: Palma, also known as Palma de Mallorca, is the capital and largest city of the autonomous community of the Balearic Islands in Spain.
It is situated on the south coast of Mallorca on the Bay of Palma.
With a population of 438,234 as of 2024, it is the 8th-largest city in Spain.

Palma,[a] also known as Palma de Mallorca (officially between 1983–1988, 2006–2008, and 2012–2016),[5] is the capital and largest city of the autonomous community of the Balearic Islands in Spain.[6] It is situated on the south coast of Mallorca on the Bay of Palma. With a population of 438,234 as of 2024, it is the 8th-largest city in Spain.
